steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $66{x}^{2}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Both terms have $x^2$, so add their coefficients:
$$60x^2+6x^2=(60+6)x^2=66x^2$$

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• A) $360x^2$: would come from multiplying $60 \times 6$, not adding. Eliminate.
  • B) $10x^2$: would come from $60/6$, not addition. Eliminate.
  • C) $54x^2$: would come from $60-6$, not addition. Eliminate.
  • D) $66x^2$: equals $60+6=66$ times $x^2$. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
